title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC will play its NWSL opener against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
More than half of Boston Legacy FC's roster has never played in the NWSL, and coach Filipa Patão is new to the league.
Gotham FC, featuring stars like Rose Lavelle, won the 2025 NWSL title, posing a strong challenge for Legacy FC.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating legislation (S.2157) to end twice-yearly clock changes, led by Senate President Karen E. Spilka.
The proposed bill, filed by State Senator John F. Keenan, suggests adopting Atlantic Standard Time to minimize health risks and increase productivity.
Research links clock changes to health issues like heart attacks and strokes; the bill is under Senate review after moving to the rules committee.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announced the 2026 parade and festival for Saturday, June 6.
The parade starts at Copley Square, ending at Boston Common with a festival and block party.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the efforts to promote inclusivity and encouraged regional and national participation.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ordered the release of a 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough.
Elected officials criticized ICE for allegedly using the girl to locate her father, while DHS claimed she was rescued.
The girl received a notice to appear in immigration court and her lawyer is seeking legal residency for her.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins struggle with scoring post-Olympics, holding the final wild-card spot in the Eastern Conference by one point.
Top players like David Pastrnak and Elias Lindholm underperform in 5-on-5 play, prompting lineup reassessment by coach Marco Sturm.
Bruins' power play success drops to 14.3% in the last eight games, contributing to their scoring challenges.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a researcher at Harvard Medical School, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for trauma survivors.
Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center at Brigham and Women's Hospital by participating in the marathon.
The Swiss student's work focuses on neurorehabilitation, helping patients regain movement and independence after serious injuries.

title: Fire destroys Newbury home, kills pets as family escapes
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A fire destroyed a Newbury home early Friday, leaving four residents unharmed but killing a dog and cat.
Firefighters from multiple towns assisted in extinguishing the blaze at 49 Fruit St., which prompted a second alarm.
The cause of the fire is under investigation by Newbury police and the Massachusetts State Police Fire and Explosion Investigation Unit.

title: How a Cambridge home cook built a 1,000-member recipe club around local ingredients
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Andrew Little founded the Cambridge Recipe Club to connect locals through recipes centered on Massachusetts ingredients.
The club has grown to over 1,000 members, mainly from Greater Boston, receiving weekly recipe newsletters.
Little collaborates with club members and local chefs, sharing both his and their recipes and stories.

title: Campbell to continue suit against Live Nation, as internal messages show employee mocked customers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell to continue antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation despite DOJ settlement.
Internal Slack messages reveal Live Nation employee mocked customers, sparking further scrutiny.
Live Nation controls over 265 venues; Ticketmaster handles 80% of major concert ticketing, per Campbell's office.

title: Healey, Campbell launch portal for reporting ICE misconduct
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Massachusetts launched a portal for reporting ICE misconduct, announced by Gov. Maura Healey and AG Andrea Campbell.
The portal accepts reports of misconduct by ICE, Customs and Border Protection, and others under Trump's immigration policy.
Healey and Campbell criticize ICE tactics as spreading fear; DHS defends ICE conduct as professional and necessary.
